summary: Draft, closed-unmerged WIP PR touching fighter, ground, item, library audio, and menu code. The PR body says 0 matched and 5 failed pending targets, while the bot report for commit range f50d7f6..1d8fa0d reports +2200 matched-code bytes, 13 new matches, and 18 unmatched-item improvements. The diff replaces many placeholder stubs with C bodies, updates several signatures/types/headers, introduces some data/type guesses, and uses narrow matching tactics such as pragmas and PAD_STACK.

key_files.role: Largest changed file; added grHr_804D6AE4 and four near-identical grHomeRun_* text-positioning function bodies reported as 100% matches.
evidence_refs: diff: grHomeRun_8021DF50, grHomeRun_8021E0D4, grHomeRun_8021E258, grHomeRun_8021E3DC bodies

what_changed: Replaced placeholder comments with recovered C bodies across fighter, ground, item, library, and menu translation units.

Bot report says 13 symbols became 100% matched, including grCorneria_801E0F6C, grCorneria_801E2D90, grCorneria_801E0D30, four grHomeRun functions, fn_800DC070, ft_800C0098, it_2725_Logic101_Destroyed, ftCh_Init_80157170, lbAudioAx_80024DC4, and grOldPupupu_80210C7C.

Several other functions improved but did not match according to the bot report, including ftKb_SpecialN_800F1420, fn_801FBF6C, grOldKongo_8021005C, grGreatBay_801F660C, it_802E1E94, it_2725_Logic101_ShieldBounced, ittools functions, and mnName_8023749C.
Updated prototypes and types in headers: grGreatBay_801F660C, grHomeRun_8021EC58, ftCrazyHandAttributes.x24, grHomeRun ground vars, and itMewtwoShadowball item vars.
Introduced or modified data/section-related source, including grGb_803E4048, grHr_804D6AE4, .sdata2/.sbss effects in grhomerun and itklap, .data changes in grgreatbay, and .bss changes in lbaudio_ax.
Used explicit matching tactics in some bodies: #pragma auto_inline off/global_optimizer off around ft_800C0098 and PAD_STACK(16) in fn_801FBF6C.

smart_moves.text: The PR updates owning headers when bodies recover signatures, e.g. grGreatBay_801F660C and grHomeRun_8021EC58, rather than only adding source-local declarations.

The PR recovers typed union/struct fields for some item and ground accesses, such as itMewtwoShadowball_ItemVars.x2C as HSD_GObj* and grHomeRun_GroundVars, instead of leaving all accesses through generic unknown storage.
The nested loops in ftKb_SpecialN_800F1420 and the loop over Inishie blocks express repeated indexed traversal naturally rather than duplicating generated repeated blocks.

decomp_lessons.lesson: A bot report can contradict or supersede stale WIP body text; preserve both. Here the PR body says 0 matched and 5 failed, while the bot reports 13 new matches and 18 improvements for the tested commit range.
Typed layout refinements can be part of matching progress when new bodies expose field roles, but in a draft closed-unmerged PR they should be treated as candidates requiring curator/source review.
Pragmas and PAD_STACK appeared as narrow tactics tied to specific functions, not global style. They need verifier evidence and cleanup review before promotion.
evidence_refs: diff: ft_800C0098 wrapped in #pragma auto_inline off and #pragma global_optimizer off

decomp_lessons.lesson: Near-identical matched bodies, such as the four grHomeRun_* functions, are useful search targets for checking whether the original source used repeated authored copies, a helper, or a macro; the PR evidence shows repeated bodies but does not prove original intent.
evidence_refs: diff: grHomeRun_8021DF50, grHomeRun_8021E0D4, grHomeRun_8021E258, grHomeRun_8021E3DC nearly identical

naming_conventions.name: fp, gp, ip, gobj, jobj, dobj, mobj, mat
naming_conventions.context: Short local role names are used for Fighter, Ground, Item, GObj, JObj, DObj, MObj, and material variables.
evidence_refs: diff: ftKb_SpecialN_800F1420 uses fp, dobj, mobj, mat

naming_conventions.name: address-style function names retained
naming_conventions.context: Functions without proven semantic names remain as fn_*/module_address names, e.g. fn_800DC070, ft_800C0098, fn_801FBF6C, grOldKongo_8021005C.
evidence_refs: diff: multiple recovered bodies retain address-style names

naming_conventions.name: xNN unknown field names retained where semantics are unclear
naming_conventions.context: Recovered structs and vars mostly keep x-offset-style fields such as xC4, xC6, xC8, xCC, x24, x2C, preserving uncertainty.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: The function is wrapped with #pragma push, #pragma auto_inline off, #pragma global_optimizer off, then #pragma pop. Bot reports ft_800C0098 as a 100% new match.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK in a high-percent but unmatched function
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: fn_801FBF6C ends with PAD_STACK(16); bot reports 93.72%, so the tactic did not produce a full match in the loaded report.
ftKb_SpecialN_800F1420 uses nested for-loops over lookup entries and per-entry bytes; fn_801FBF6C uses a for-loop over 19 blocks; grGreatBay_801F660C loops over seven table rows.
The PR changes UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete signatures for recovered functions and adjusts field types/union arms used by the new bodies.
grGreatBay adds grGb_803E4048 and a loop over it; bot reports the function at 99.08% and .data improvement, so data ownership remains review-sensitive.

notes: No human review comments or review approvals are present in the loaded slice.
The PR was closed without merge according to metadata, so no change should be treated as accepted project history solely from this slice.
The PR body appears stale relative to the bot report: it claims 0 matched and lists failed pending targets, while the bot reports 13 new matches for the tested commit range.
The loaded raw diff is truncated, so details for later files such as ittools.c, lbaudio_ax.c, mnname.c, and some shadowball changes are incomplete.
Bot report is useful verifier evidence for the tested commit range but does not provide reviewer intent or prove long-term acceptance.
Some changes touch data sections and type layouts; the slice gives match/improvement numbers but not enough ownership evidence to promote those layouts as accepted facts.

accepted_candidate_records.title: WIP PR body may be stale relative to bot match report
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2315, the author body reports 0 matched and 5 failed pending targets, but the decomp-dev bot report for f50d7f6..1d8fa0d reports 13 new 100% matches and 18 improvements. Intake records should preserve both sources and avoid treating the body as final validation.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Pragmas can appear in matched WIP bodies but remain review-sensitive
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2315 reports ft_800C0098 as a 100% new match while the diff wraps it in #pragma auto_inline off and #pragma global_optimizer off. Because the PR is draft/closed-unmerged and standards treat pragmas as exceptions, this is evidence for a tactic case, not an accepted style rule.

source_update_candidates.title: Candidate grHomeRun ground vars layout from PR #2315
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2315 adds struct grHomeRun_GroundVars with u16 xC4, u16 xC6, int xC8, int xCC and adds a homerun union arm in Ground. The new grHomeRun_* functions use gp->gv.homerun.xC6 while also accessing gp->gv.unk.xC8/xCC. Because the PR is closed-unmerged and the repeated bodies/data changes need review, treat this only as a candidate layout fact.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential typed-field recovery relevant to future grhomerun work, but not accepted by this unmerged PR.

source_update_candidates.title: Candidate Mewtwo Shadow Ball item var x2C owner pointer
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2315 changes itMewtwoShadowball_ItemVars at ip+E00 from f32 x2C[6] to HSD_GObj* x2C plus f32 x30[5]. The added Destroyed logic compares x2C to ip->owner and calls Mewtwo/Kirby special-N reset helpers before nulling x2C. Treat as a candidate item-var layout/role fact pending source review.
source_update_candidates.reason: The code strongly suggests an owner/fighter GObj pointer role, but the PR was not merged.

source_update_candidates.title: Candidate ftCrazyHandAttributes x24 float type
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2315 changes ftCrazyHandAttributes.x24 from s32 to float and uses attrs->x24 as the y component in ftCh_Init_80157170, which the bot reports as a new 100% match. Treat as candidate type information only.
source_update_candidates.reason: Verifier evidence supports the local function, but source acceptance is not shown.

source_update_candidates.title: No new standard needed: PR #2315 reinforces existing pragma/PAD_STACK caution
source_update_candidates.text: The slice contains #pragma auto_inline off/global_optimizer off around a reported 100% match and PAD_STACK in a 93.72% function. Existing standards already cover this under matching-tactics-need-evidence and avoid-pragmas-register-asm.
source_update_candidates.reason: Route as a reinforcement/case example rather than a new rule.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the PR body's 0-matched status as the final validation result without also recording the bot report that found 13 new matches.
Do not promote any changed type or data layout as accepted path fact; the PR is closed-unmerged and lacks human review evidence.
Do not infer reviewer intent or merge acceptability; no review comments or reviews are loaded.
Do not treat the repeated grHomeRun bodies as proof that the original source was intentionally copy-pasted; the evidence only shows repeated recovered bodies and bot match results.
Do not treat pragmas or PAD_STACK from this WIP slice as normal decomp style.
